support.title: Support
support.description: Describe your issue — we'll reply to the e-mail in your profile.
support.subject: Subject
support.sent: Message sent. We'll reply to your email.

marketing.heroSubtitle: Banana, Veo, Kling and dozens more behind one stable contract. Predictable USD pricing, idempotent requests, queue and callbacks — out of the box.
marketing.featuresSubtitle: Not a thin SDK wrapper, but a full gateway: billing, queues, retries, callbacks, analytics.
marketing.feature1Description: Banana, Veo, Kling and others through one stable contract. Switch providers without rewriting clients.
marketing.feature2Description: Transparent per-request pricing. No vendor tokens, no hidden margins.
marketing.feature3Description: Default tariff, personal tariff or individual prices per bundle.
marketing.feature4Description: Idempotent, async, with task state and automatic webhook delivery.
marketing.feature5Description: HMAC-signed payloads, exponential backoff retries, DLQ for incident review.
marketing.feature6Description: Transaction log, secret rotation, key isolation, RBAC for teams.

codeExample.title: One curl, end-to-end
codeExample.subtitle: One contract for every method. Drop a job in the queue, get a taskId, fetch the result via callback or polling.
codeExample.bullet2: Sync for fast methods, async for long ones

pricing.title: Pay only for what you use
pricing.subtitle: No subscriptions, no monthly minimum. Top up your balance in USD and spend as you go.

cta.title: Ship AI in an hour, not a week

footer.tagline: Single API gateway over the leading AI providers.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Auto-generated reference for every provider, model and method.
docs.introBody: A unified API gateway for AI image and video providers. Browse methods, integrate against /v1, and pay in real USD. Pricing is private to your account — see your dashboard.
docs.methodsIndexBody: Browse providers, models and methods supported by the API. Click a provider to drill down into its models.
docs.noMethods: No methods configured for this model.
docs.countModels: {count, plural, =0 {no models} one {# model} other {# models}}
docs.countMethods: {count, plural, =0 {no methods} one {# method} other {# methods}}
docs.gettingStartedBody: Five steps from zero to your first generation.
docs.gsStep1: Register an account and verify your email.
docs.gsStep2: Create an API key in your dashboard. The plaintext is shown once — store it safely.
docs.gsStep3: Top up your balance via the Top-up page.
docs.gsStep4: Call POST /v1/estimate to preview the cost of a request.
docs.gsStep5: Call POST /v1/generations with an Idempotency-Key header to start a generation.
docs.authenticationBody: Pass your API key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Keys have the form sk_live_<prefix>_<secret>.
docs.authBodyExtra: Keep keys server-side only. If a key is exposed, revoke it immediately and create a new one.
docs.errorsBody: All errors share a stable error.code. The public envelope is { success: false, error: { code, message, details, request_id } }.
docs.idempotencyBody: The Idempotency-Key header lets you safely retry the same POST /v1/generations after network errors or timeouts. The replayed request returns the exact same response without creating a second task or charging twice.
docs.idempotencyHowBody: Generate a unique key on the client (UUID or your own business identifier) and pass it in the Idempotency-Key header. Tie the key to the entity you're protecting from duplicate processing (e.g. order ID + attempt number).
docs.idempotencyKeyFormatScope: Scope: public_api.generations. The same key cannot be reused across different endpoints with different bodies.
docs.idempotencyOutcomeReplay: Same key + same body + previous request finished → cached response is returned (same task_id, same HTTP status).
docs.idempotencyOutcomeInflight: Same key + same body + previous request still in flight → idempotency_key_in_use error. Wait 1–2 seconds and retry.
docs.idempotencyOutcomeMismatch: Same key + different body → idempotency_key_mismatch. Use a new key.
docs.idempotencyTtlBody: Cached responses live for 24 hours after completion; the in-flight guard is 30 seconds. After 24h the key can be reused, but you should generate a new one.
docs.idempotencyAdviceUuid: Use UUIDv4 or a tuple (entity_id × attempt_number). Don't put secrets in the key — it gets logged.
docs.idempotencyAdviceRecord: Persist the key in your own DB at task creation time so retries can reuse the right key.
docs.idempotencyAdviceNoDouble: Never send two requests with the same key but different parameters — you'll get 409 mismatch and waste time debugging.
docs.webhooksBody: If you pass callback_url to POST /v1/generations, our worker will POST to that URL the moment the task reaches a terminal state (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or CANCELLED).
docs.webhooksHowBody: Pass callback_url in the request body (HTTPS required). The URL is stored on the ApiRequest and cannot be changed after task creation.
docs.webhooksPayloadNotes: result is non-null only when status=succeeded; error is non-null only on failed/cancelled. charged_amount is in nano-USD (1 USD = 100,000,000 units).
docs.webhooksSignatureBody: The signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the raw (not re-serialised) request body using your API key's webhook secret. Compare using timing-safe equality.
docs.webhooksRetry2xx: Any 2xx response counts as successful delivery; everything else is retried.
docs.webhooksRetryAttempts: Up to 5 attempts with exponential backoff (BullMQ).
docs.webhooksRetryTimeout: Response timeout is 10 seconds. If your server takes longer, return 200 immediately and process asynchronously.
docs.webhooksRetryIdempotent: Your handler must be idempotent: treat task_id as the primary key and ignore duplicates.
docs.webhooksDebugBody: The full delivery history is visible in admin (Webhook Deliveries section) with payload, signature, HTTP status, and response body per attempt. If callback_url is not provided, no callback is sent — poll GET /v1/tasks/{id} instead.
docs.taskLifecycleBody: A task ends in one of three terminal states: SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or CANCELLED. Before that it's PENDING (queued) or PROCESSING (adapter executing).
docs.taskLifecycleStatePending: Accepted, waiting for a free provider account in the BullMQ queue.
docs.taskLifecycleStateProcessing: Account selected, adapter is calling the upstream provider.
docs.taskLifecycleStateSucceeded: Files were generated and uploaded successfully. result is populated.
docs.taskLifecycleStateCancelled: Task was cancelled (by user or by system on timeout).
docs.taskLifecyclePollingBody: If callback_url is not set, poll GET /v1/tasks/{id}. Recommended interval: 2 seconds for image generation, 5–10 seconds for video.
docs.taskLifecyclePollAdvice1: Don't poll more than once per second or you may hit rate_limit_exceeded.
docs.taskLifecyclePollAdvice2: Stop polling once status is SUCCEEDED, FAILED, or CANCELLED — those are terminal.
docs.taskLifecyclePollAdvice3: Prefer webhooks over polling when possible — they're more reliable and cheaper.
docs.taskLifecycleSuccessUrlNote: result.url is the primary file (PNG/JPEG for image methods). Download with a plain GET — no auth required.
docs.taskLifecycleSuccessFilesNote: result.files is an array of all generated files (up to 4 for multi-image methods). result.url duplicates the first entry for convenience.
docs.taskLifecycleSuccessExpiryNote: result.available_until — after this date the file is removed from storage. Download and persist on your side within that window.
docs.taskLifecycleFailureRetryable: provider_outage and service_unavailable are transient — retry after 30–60s with exponential backoff.
docs.taskLifecycleFailureNonRetryable: provider_rejected, content_rejected, and invalid_parameters won't recover on a blind retry — fix parameters.
docs.taskLifecycleCancelBody: Cancel via DELETE /v1/tasks/{id} while the task is PENDING or PROCESSING. After SUCCEEDED cancellation is not possible. Refunds happen automatically for FAILED/CANCELLED.

errorsCode.invalid_api_key: API key is invalid or revoked.
errorsCode.insufficient_balance: Wallet balance is below the required amount.
errorsCode.price_not_configured: No price is configured for this method/bundle.
errorsCode.method_not_available_for_user: This method is not enabled for your account.
errorsCode.task_not_owned: You do not own this task.
errorsCode.invalid_parameters: Some parameters did not pass validation.
errorsCode.rate_limit_exceeded: You hit the rate limit. Slow down and retry.
errorsCode.idempotency_key_in_use: Idempotency key is currently being processed.
errorsCode.idempotency_key_mismatch: Idempotency key was reused with a different payload.
errorsCode.internal_error: Unexpected internal error. Try again later.
errorsCode.service_unavailable: Service is temporarily unavailable. Retry in a few seconds.
errorsCode.provider_outage: No provider capacity available right now. Retry in 30–60 seconds.
errorsCode.provider_rejected: The provider rejected the request. Adjust parameters before retrying — a blind retry will not succeed.
errorsCode.content_rejected: The provider's safety filter rejected the prompt or input images.
